name: Enrion
bio:
Physical Appearance: Altmer (High Elf) male. Golden-skinned with the features of his race. Wears simple, practical clothing - a quilted doublet, tan linens, and doeskin shoes - the modest attire of someone living as a fugitive rather than the finery typically associated with High Elves.
Race: Altmer
Personality Keywords: paranoid, desperate, fugitive, aggressive, fearful
Role: Enrion is a mage and a fugitive from justice who has taken refuge in Bloodmayne Cave outside of Bravil. He lives in constant fear of being captured and returned to prison, which has made him hostile and dangerous to anyone who discovers his hideout.
Current Situation: Enrion has made Bloodmayne Cave his hideout, residing in the second zone alongside fellow fugitive Dreet-Lai. Together they wander the cave, presumably planning their next move while relying on other associates - Hlofgar and Ashanta - who guard the entrance to protect them from discovery. His life is one of hiding and paranoia, reacting violently to any perceived threat.
Temperament: Enrion attacks anyone who trespasses into his hideout on sight. His aggression stems from fear - he is terrified of being captured and hauled back to prison. This desperation has made him willing to kill to preserve his freedom, viewing any intruder as a potential threat to his safety.
Combat Abilities: Despite his fugitive status, Enrion remains a capable mage. He wields standard leveled Mage spells appropriate to his skill and carries an enchanted, leveled dagger as backup - both his magical and physical combat capabilities are maintained at his highest skill levels, making him a dangerous opponent for those who stumble upon his hiding place. (Note: Due to his extreme paranoia and immediate hostility toward intruders, Enrion does not engage in conversation. Any encounter with him results in combat as he fights desperately to avoid capture and return to prison.)
author: Bethesda
